Site Reliability Engineer
CloudScale Inc. — Remote (US/EU timezones)

About CloudScale Inc.
CloudScale Inc. is a cloud infrastructure platform serving 500+ enterprise customers. Our SRE team owns reliability, scalability, and operational excellence across a distributed microservices architecture running on Kubernetes at scale.

About the Role
We are looking for an experienced Site Reliability Engineer to join our platform team. You will be responsible for designing and maintaining the infrastructure that keeps our systems running at 99.99% uptime. You will own oncall rotations, respond to incidents, and drive post-mortem culture.

Responsibilities
- Design, implement, and maintain Kubernetes clusters (EKS) across multiple regions
- Define and track Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and manage SLO budgets and error budgets for critical services
- Participate in oncall rotations (1 week per 6 weeks), lead incident response and write post-mortems
- Build and maintain infrastructure-as-code using Terraform for cloud resource provisioning (AWS)
- Implement and maintain observability stack: Prometheus for metrics collection, Grafana dashboards, and PagerDuty for alerting and incident management
- Drive cost optimization initiatives across AWS services; identify and eliminate waste in compute and storage spend
- Collaborate with development teams to establish SLIs, SLOs, and error budget policies
- Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for infrastructure deployments

Requirements
- 3+ years of SRE or DevOps experience in a production environment
- Deep expertise with Kubernetes (cluster administration, RBAC, networking, autoscaling)
- Proficiency with Terraform for infrastructure-as-code
- Experience with Prometheus, Grafana, and production metrics infrastructure
- Hands-on experience with PagerDuty or similar incident management platform
- Proven track record of oncall rotations and incident management at scale
- Experience defining and managing SLO budgets and error budgets
- Strong understanding of cost optimization in cloud environments (AWS preferred)
- Experience with containerization (Docker) and microservices architectures
- Scripting proficiency: Python or Go preferred; bash required
- Familiarity with gRPC for service-to-service communication

Nice to Have
- Experience with service meshes (Istio, Linkerd)
- Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA)
- Experience with chaos engineering tools (Gremlin, Chaos Monkey)

Compensation
$150,000–$185,000 base salary + equity + benefits
